summary: Iteration 19 backend regression verified: tip announcements no longer consume the 3s shoutbox rate-limit slot. 4/4 pytest cases pass against public URL.
tests_executed: test_tip_then_normal_msg_not_ratelimited — tip immediately followed by normal msg returns 200 (previously 429); consecutive 2nd normal msg still 429 with 'slow down' detail
test_two_tips_within_3s_both_succeed — two tips within <3s both return 200
test_tip_after_normal_msg_still_works — tip immediately after a normal msg returns 200
critical_code_review_comments: Fix is well-scoped: rate-limit lookup filter {'kind': {'$ne': 'tip'}} correctly excludes tip announcements while still recording them into public_chat for UI display.
Tips endpoint itself has no explicit rate-limit — relies on balance/economic cost as the natural throttle. Acceptable but worth a comment in code.
seed_data_creation: None — tests use existing testbugfix1 balance (~$13). Each run consumes ~$2 in tips to Balkin.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_iter19_tip_rate_bypass.py is idempotent-ish but each run tips $2 from testbugfix1 to Balkin — monitor balance for long-term reruns. Rate-window state is normalized via 4s sleeps at start of each test.
